rationale: The citing paper references Pauwels, Hanssens, and Siddarth (2002) not only as part of the dynamic pricing literature but also to support a core modeling premise: sales are typically stationary in FMCG settings, so temporary promotions should not have permanent effects. That empirical premise is used to justify the paper's error-correction setup and its emphasis on cumulative (rather than permanent) promotion effects. Because this citation helps motivate key conceptual and modeling choices, it is more than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited paper as prior empirical grounding for its assumption that temporary promotions do not permanently shift sales levels in stationary markets. This support underlies the citing paper's ECM formulation and its immediate-versus-cumulative effect framing.
